title: Detecting pump suction, pump thrombus, and other adverse vad motor events
abstract: A controller for an implantable blood pump including processing circuitry configured to operate the implantable blood pump and a piezoelectric element in communication with the implantable blood pump.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A controller for an implantable blood pump, comprising: processing circuitry configured to operate the implantable blood pump; and a piezoelectric element in communication with the implantable blood pump. 2. The controller of Claim 1, wherein the controller includes a header block, and wherein the piezoelectric element is included in the header block. 3. The controller of Claim 2, wherein the controller includes a driveline in communication with the implantable blood pump, and wherein the piezoelectric element is configured to sense vibrations from the driveline when the implantable blood pump is operating. 4. The controller of Claim 3, wherein the controller is configured to correlate the sensed vibrations into a determination of at least one from the group consisting of a presence and absence of thrombus. 5. The controller of Claim 4, wherein the controller is configured to generate an alert if the presence of thrombus is determined. 6. The controller of Claim 1, wherein the piezoelectric element is a crystal. 7. The controller of Claim 1, wherein the controller includes a driveline in communication with the implantable blood pump, and wherein the piezoelectric element is coupled to the driveline. 8. The controller of Claim 1, wherein the controller is configured to be implanted within a body of a patient. 9. An implantable blood pump system, comprising: a blood pump configured to be implanted within a portion of a mammalian heart; and a controller according to Claim 2 coupled to the blood pump by driveline. 10. The system of Claim 9, wherein the controller includes a header block, and wherein the piezoelectric element is coupled to the header block. 11. The system of Claim 10, wherein the controller includes processing circuitry configured to sense vibrations from the piezoelectric element and correlate the sensed vibrations into a determination of at least one from the group consisting of a presence and absence of thrombus.
